channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Allstate Sued by Oklahoma for Alleged Scheme to Underpay Claims
item.description: In Oklahoma, Drummond claims that third-party engineering firms and adjusting companies participated in the alleged scheme with Allstate, providing reports that supported predetermined objectives to deny or reduce claims. Oklahoma’s attorney general sued Allstate Corp., alleging the insurer schemed to minimize payouts to homeowners for wind and hailstorm damage in order to boost its profits. The state’s top lawyer, Gentner Drummond, alleges that Allstate implemented an internal program to drastically deny or minimize payments of legitimate covered losses, according to the lawsuit filed Tuesday in state court in Cleveland.

item.title: Gift City’s new MGA regime a ‘build-or-broker’ moment for Indian insurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: SoftBank forms SB Neo to launch AI cloud business in the US
item.description: The announcement follows SoftBank’s broader push into AI infrastructure, including plans to launch its AI Data Center GPU Cloud service in October 2026 and develop gigawatt-scale AI data centers in Japan. and SoftBank Group have established SB Neo, a new US-based company that will provide AI cloud infrastructure to enterprise customers. SoftBank said the initiative builds on its AI cloud efforts in Japan, where it began offering a beta version of its GPU cloud service powered by its Infrinia AI Cloud OS software stack in May 2026.

item.title: Principal to acquire Beam Benefits
item.description: Principal Financial Group announced an agreement to acquire Beam Benefits, an employee benefits company serving over 25,000 small businesses. Beam’s talent and deep expertise in the small business marketplace will be additive to our SMB strategy.” – Amy Friedrich, president of Benefits and Protection at Principal. Joining forces with Principal is the natural next step in our journey.” – Tolithia Kornweibel, CEO of Beam Benefits.

item.title: APCO Holdings acquires Georgia Wheelz
item.description: APCO Holdings has acquired the assets of Georgia Wheelz, an F&I agency serving independent automotive dealers across the Southeast. Following the acquisition, the Georgia Wheelz team will join APCO’s GWC Warranty sales organization, expanding GWC’s presence in the independent dealer market. Founded in 2017 and based in Eatonton, Georgia, Georgia Wheelz provides F&I products, dealer training, and performance support to independent dealerships.

item.title: Duck Creek Acquires Send
item.description: (Image source: Send homepage.) Duck Creek Technologies (Boston) has acquired Send Technology Solutions Ltd. Duck Creek says the acquisition brings together its core insurance platform and Agentic AI Platform with Send’s AI-native underwriting orchestration engine, creating a more connected underwriting-to-core operating model for commercial, specialty and London Market insurers. Supporting Complex Commercial and Specialty Underwriting Duck Creek says Send supports more than $26 billion in gross written premium through its underwriting orchestration engine.

item.title: Meta Says States Seeking $1.4 Trillion in Penalties in August Youth Safety Trial
item.description: Meta Platforms said in a court filing on Monday that four states were seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties over accusations the company designed its Facebook and Instagram platforms to addict young users and misled the public about their safety. Meta put forward the figure in its response to the attorneys general’s filings on how penalties should be calculated if the states prevailed at trial. The number, which has not previously been disclosed and is close to Meta’s market capitalization of around $1.5 trillion, comes ahead of an August trial in Oakland, California, over the claims brought by California, Colorado, Kentucky and New Jersey against the company.

item.title: Global Cargo Theft Ring Charged in $10M Scheme
item.description: Eight individuals were charged with conspiracy to transport and possess stolen property in connection with an international, organized scheme to steal cargo from commercial shippers, according to a joint announcement last week by the United States Attorney for the Southern District of New York and the New York Field Office of the Federal Bureau of Investigation. From March 2023 to the present, the defendants carried out a large-scale, organized scheme to steal cargo from commercial shippers. “Organized cargo theft is an attack on the integrity of our nation’s commercial supply chain and our markets more generally.

item.title: U.S. Proposes Dropping Brake Pedal Requirements for Self-Driving Vehicles
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration proposed on Thursday to end a government requirement for manual brake pedals in self-driving vehicles, a move that would make it easier to deploy such vehicles on U.S. The proposal would not apply to vehicles with human driver controls and NHTSA said it would not drop braking performance requirements, including strict stopping distance standards for self-driving vehicles. Under the law, ⁠fully self-driving vehicles do not need NHTSA approval if they have required human controls, like steering wheels, brake pedals or mirrors.

item.title: Sompo Acquiring Workers Comp Writer Service Insurance Companies
item.description: According to the announcement, the acquisition of Service Insurance Companies will broaden Sompo’s underwriting capabilities and presence across products, customer segments and distribution channels within the small and medium-sized enterprise segment, which is a focus for workers comp specialist being acquired. “Together with Service Insurance Companies’ seasoned leadership team and proven workers compensation platform, Sompo is establishing a top-tier flagship for our workers compensation business,” said Chris Sparro, CEO, North America, Sompo Commercial P&C Insurance, in a media statement. “Together with Sompo, Service Insurance Companies is better positioned to extend the reach from a monoline workers compensation provider into a diversified commercial insurance organization with enhanced scale, capabilities and amplified market relevance,” Davis said.

item.title: Sompo swoops for US workers’ comp specialist Service Insurance Companies
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon appoints Reville as MD, Casualty Catastrophe Market Leader for Risk Capital
item.description: Global broker Aon has announced that it has appointed Bob Reville as Senior Managing Director and Casualty Catastrophe Market Leader within its Risk Capital division, effective July 15th. This is an exciting step forward in strengthening how we bring together casualty expertise, analytics, and market innovation to solve increasingly complex client challenges.” Addressing his new role, Reville said: “We are building a casualty catastrophe market for latent risk that can match the property catastrophe market, expanding coverage and opening new opportunities across the insurance and capital markets. Aon’s ability to connect capital to risk supports this ambition, and I’m excited to work alongside such entrepreneurial, client-focused colleagues.” In the past, Reville has noted that the capital markets has historically avoided the casualty space due to the extremely long-tail nature of the risks, which can tie up invested capital for decades.

item.title: Long-term El Niño (ENSO) correlation holds little predictive power for annual property losses: KCC
item.description: According to a recent paper by catastrophe risk modeler Karen Clark & Company (KCC), while a long-term correlation exists between El Niño/Southern Oscillation (ENSO) phases and insured property losses, the climate pattern holds relatively little predictive power for any single year. The El Niño/Southern Oscillation (ENSO) is a climate pattern that involves sea-surface temperature changes in the eastern tropical Pacific Ocean. In its paper, KCC observes that sea-surface temperatures in the tropical Eastern Pacific have become anomalously warm in recent months, which heavily indicates the El Niño phase of ENSO.

item.title: Correa promoted to President & CEO of Munich Re Canada (Life)
item.description: Global reinsurer Munich Re has announced the appointment of Michael Correa as President and CEO of its Canada (Life) business, effective September 1, 2026. His appointment to lead our Life & Health business in Europe and Latin America speaks to the strength of our global leadership team.” Correa, who currently serves as Senior Vice President, Reinsurance Solutions for Munich Re, Canada (Life), added: “I am honoured to take on this role and to lead the Canada (Life) business at such an important time. In his new position, based in Toronto, Correa will be responsible for the Life business in Canada and the Caribbean, and will be reporting to Mari-Lizette Malherbe, Member of the Board of Management.

item.title: More favourable reinsurance conditions strengthen insurers ahead of 2026 hurricane season: KBRA
item.description: KBRA, the US-based global credit ratings agency, says that more favourable reinsurance conditions are providing insurers with stronger protection ahead of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, despite warning that a quieter seasonal outlook does not remove the threat of significant catastrophe losses. January, April, and mid-year 2026 renewal reports from brokers have highlighted that abundant capacity from both traditional reinsurers and insurance-linked securities (ILS) investors has driven more favourable renewal outcomes for cedents, with KBRA-rated insurers reporting property catastrophe reinsurance price reductions of between 10% and 25% at the June 2026 renewals. While forecasts indicate below-average Atlantic hurricane activity in 2026, KBRA stresses that seasonal projections cannot predict landfall, meaning a single hurricane affecting a heavily insured coastal region could still result in substantial insured losses.

item.title: MS Re hires Lisa Butera as CUO for NA and expands Lucas Castagnino’s leadership role
item.description: Switzerland-based reinsurer MS Reinsurance has hired Lisa Butera from Swiss Re to serve as Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO) for North America (NA), and has also expanded Lucas Castagnino’s role to CUO for Latin America and Caribbean. Lisa’s and Lucas’s experience, leadership skills and perspectives will help us build on the momentum we have already created.” “These appointments follow a comprehensive search process, which included both internal and external candidates, highlighting the strength and depth of talent within MS Re’s underwriting organization. Butera brings more than three decades of leadership experience across insurance and reinsurance markets to MS Re.

item.title: Sompo to Acquire Service Insurance Companies to Expand US Workers Comp
item.description: said it has a definitive agreement to acquire Service Insurance Companies, a monoline workers’ compensation insurance company. “Together with Service Insurance Companies’ seasoned leadership team and proven workers’ compensation platform, Sompo is establishing a top-tier flagship for our workers’ compensation business,” said Chris Sparro, CEO, North America, Sompo Commercial P&C Insurance. “Together with Sompo, Service Insurance Companies is better positioned to extend the reach from a monoline workers’ compensation provider into a diversified commercial insurance organization with enhanced scale, capabilities and amplified market relevance,” he said.

item.title: NTSB Finds No Engine Failure in Deadly Missouri Skydiving Plane Crash
item.description: Federal safety investigators said in a new preliminary report that they found no indication that engine failure caused the fiery crash of a plane on a skydiving outing last month in Missouri that killed all 12 people aboard , including several very experienced jumpers. In fact, NTSB said it appeared that the engine of the single-engine turboprop plane had been producing power at the time of the crash. The report said there were no indications of any precrash mechanical malfunctions or failures in the engine that would have prevented the normal operation of the plane.

item.title: Judge Approves $46.75 Million Payout for 23andMe Data Breach Victims
item.description: bankruptcy judge on July 7 approved a $46.75 million settlement for victims of a 2023 data breach at the genetic testing company 23andMe, which exposed genetic and other personal information of an estimated 6.9 million customers. The settlement will be reduced by $14.29 million previously disbursed in connection with the breach, making the total additional payout $32.46 million. Related: Claimants of 23andMe Data Breach to Get $46.75M in Settlement Deal Based in Palo Alto, California, 23andMe filed for protection from creditors in March 2025.

item.title: Bowtie Crosses US$100 Million in ARR, Reaching “Centaur” Status Seven Years After Founding
item.description: The post Bowtie Crosses US$100 Million in ARR, Reaching “Centaur” Status Seven Years After Founding appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Bowtie, Hong Kong’s first virtual insurer, has crossed US$100 million in Annual Recurring Revenue, reaching the technology benchmark known as “Centaur” status seven years after founding. Bowtie, Hong Kong’s first virtual insurer, has crossed US$100 million in Annual Recurring Revenue, reaching the technology benchmark known as “Centaur” status seven years after founding.

item.title: Sompo International to Acquire Service Insurance Companies, Expanding US Workers’ Compensation Platform
item.description: The post Sompo International to Acquire Service Insurance Companies, Expanding US Workers’ Compensation Platform appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Sompo International has entered a definitive agreement to acquire Service Insurance Companies, a US specialist in workers’ compensation insurance, broadening its underwriting reach across the SME and General Agents markets. Sompo International has entered a definitive agreement to acquire Service Insurance Companies, a US specialist in workers’ compensation insurance, broadening its underwriting reach across the SME and General Agents markets.

item.title: Biggest Threat Yet to Captive Insurance Agents
item.description: State Farm's announcement of a tough new compensation structure suggests that the captive model for insurance agents has finally passed a tipping point. Viewed together, they reveal something larger: carriers are increasingly questioning whether exclusive distribution remains the optimal model for growth." Gritz also neatly summarizes what, for me, is the core change that is working against captive agents: "Consumers can purchase insurance through direct channels, comparison platforms, embedded insurance experiences, independent agencies, affinity groups, digital marketplaces, MGAs, and increasingly AI-powered interfaces. Back in 2013, when Chunka Mui and I were doing some consulting work on innovation for the CEO of a top-five personal lines insurer, he was trying to rewire the compensation structure for his captive agents.

item.title: Duck Creek acquires underwriting platform Send
item.description: Duck Creek has acquired London-based Send Technology Solutions , adding AI-powered underwriting orchestration capabilities to its insurance platform. Duck Creek said the acquisition combines Send’s underwriting orchestration platform with its Intelligent Insurance Cloud and its Agentic AI Platform, connecting submission intake, risk assessment, pricing, policy issuance, and portfolio monitoring into a single workflow. Send will continue to operate as a standalone platform while integrating more closely with Duck Creek’s platform.

item.title: Zurich Australia appoints Aaron Molivas to lead consumer claims
item.description: Zurich Australia has appointed Aaron Molivas as Head of General Insurance Consumer Claims for Australia and New Zealand, a newly created role that he will assume on July 29. In his new position, Molivas will lead Zurich’s consumer general insurance claims strategy and operations, with a focus on enhancing the claims experience across the insurer’s expanding home and motor insurance portfolio. Molivas joins Zurich from Suncorp, where he serves as Head of Transformation, Consumer Insurance, leading initiatives across product, digital, customer experience, and claims.

item.title: Gallagher acquires commercial broker Wilson M. Beck Insurance Services
item.description: The post Gallagher acquires commercial broker Wilson M. Beck Insurance Services, a retail insurance brokerage serving commercial clients across Western Canada. Beck specializes in commercial insurance with expertise in construction, commercial real estate, surety bonding, hospitality, and mining.

item.title: SMV de Perú dice que el sandbox será para supervisados, pero no descarta abrirlo a otros actores
item.description: Daniel García, superintendente adjunto de investigación, desarrollo e innovación de la SMV dijo a este medio que los actores supervisados que tengan el interés de probar algún modelo novedoso no regulado serán su “primer filtro”; no obstante, “en el proceso, evidentemente podemos abrirlo”, haciendo referencia a una potencial segunda etapa de pruebas para actores no regulados. Este año, la Superintendencia del Mercado de Valores (SMV) analizará las oportunidades y beneficios de crear un sandbox regulatorio y no descarta permitir que el entorno se abra a modelos de innovación no regulados, según mencionaron a iupana representantes del regulador peruano. En su Agenda Temprana 2026-2027 , la autoridad ya había adelantado que evaluaría la necesidad del mercado por probar modelos novedosos no regulados en un espacio de pruebas que está pensado para actores bajo su perímetro de supervisión.

item.title: Accelerant Launches ARX Consortium at Lloyd’s
item.description: (Image source: Lloyd’s homepage.) Accelerant (Atlanta) has launched the ARX Consortium at Lloyd’s, backed by seven Lloyd’s syndicates. Accelerant says the ARX Consortium combines premium flow from the Accelerant Risk Exchange with capacity and market security from participating Lloyd’s syndicates. The company says the consortium enhances its relationship with Lloyd’s and creates a flexible platform for a diversified specialty risk portfolio.

item.title: Hellman & Friedman’s Hub International Seeks $3 Billion in IPO
item.description: Hellman & Friedman was among the firms backing Medline Inc., whose listing raising $7.2 billion was 2025’s largest. The Hellman & Friedman-backed company is targeting going public as soon as this year, some of the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. Representatives for Hub, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, Barclays, BMO Capital Markets and JPMorgan declined to comment.

item.title: Opening remarks on sound practices for artificial intelligence
item.description: Thank you for the opportunity to join you today to discuss the Financial Stability Board’s (FSB) consultation report on Sound Practices for Responsible Adoption of Artificial Intelligence. In setting out my priorities late last year, I initiated work on a set of sound practices for financial institutions to consider in the responsible adoption and use of Artificial Intelligence (AI). As you provide feedback, I encourage you to identify areas where the sound practices may be too prescriptive or where they fail to adequately account for differences in institutional size, complexity, and risk profile.

item.title: North American Startup Funding Shattered Records In First Half Of 2026, Driven By AI
item.description: North American venture investment hit all-time highs in the first half of 2026, driven by late-stage megarounds for AI industry leaders, Crunchbase data shows. and Canadian startups totaled a staggering $392 billion for the first half of 2026, per Crunchbase data, dwarfing anything we’ve seen before. If that introductory sentence sounds familiar, that’s because it’s the same storyline we reported for the first quarter , when OpenAI drove investment to stratospheric heights with the largest venture round of all time.

item.title: Leadenhall Capital Partners’ ILS funds back acquisition of austrion Life
item.description: Insurance-linked securities funds managed by specialist ILS investment manager Leadenhall Capital Partners LLP have backed an agreement to acquire the shares of Austrian life insurance specialist company austrion Life. SKH Funding Limited is backed economically by an investor base that consists solely of investment mandates and funds managed by Leadenhall Capital Partners, the London headquartered specialist ILS investment firm. Through its life ILS and alternative credit fund strategies Leadenhall Capital Partners has a history of supporting a range of financings for life insurers, where it sees opportunities to derive positive returns for its investors.

item.title: Third wave of speakers for our Artemis London 2026 conference
item.description: Today we have added four more expert speakers to the Artemis London 2026 website, our next catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) market conference. Artemis London 2026 will be our fifth conference focused on the catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) market to be held in the City of London. We are excited to announce four more expert speakers for Artemis London 2026, adding further depth of ILS sector experience to our roster for the day.

item.title: Parametric specialist Adaptive eyes specialty product growth following $5m funding
item.description: This funding allows us to continue expanding development and distribution of our suite of products and the technology that supports them.” Adaptive Insurance launched in 2024 as a tech-focused parametric insurance platform , with an initial power outage focused GridProtect parametric product designed to fill gaps in traditional insurance coverage. Adaptive Insurance, a climate resilience company and parametric start-up, has successfully secured an additional $5 million in financing from new and existing investors, which will be used to accelerate Adaptive’s development and distribution of specialty products designed to address the growing risks related to climate and weather events. The recent investment will reportedly support Adaptive’s momentum to expand its specialty product portfolio, grow its agent and partner distribution network, and continue development of its proprietary climate intelligence platform.

item.title: EPIC names Donatelli as National Surety Practice Development Leader
item.description: EPIC Insurance Brokers & Consultants (EPIC), a US insurance brokerage and consulting firm, has announced the appointment of Monica Donatelli as National Surety Practice Development Leader. These strategic moves reflect EPIC’s commitment to expanding its national surety practice and providing clients with enhanced resources and market expertise, the firm noted. In her new position, Donatelli will leverage her over 16 years of experience in construction and commercial surety to develop practice strategy, client service delivery and the expansion of the firm’s surety capabilities across the United States.

item.title: Catastrophe bond issuance exceeds $11.3bn in record second quarter: Artemis
item.description: After a busy opening quarter of the year, strong catastrophe bond and related insurance-linked securities (ILS) issuance momentum continued in the second quarter of 2026, with a quarterly record of more than $11.3 billion of issuance from a record 48 transactions, reports Artemis. Now available to download, the Artemis Q2 2026 Catastrophe Bond and related insurance-linked securities (ILS) Market Report examines the cat bond and related ILS risk capital issued in the record second-quarter, as well as key trends from the period, and a review of the first half . Artemis’ data shows that combined with the very active first-quarter, first-half cat bond issuance hit a new high of almost $18 billion , above last year’s previous H1 record of $17.6 billion.

item.title: Duck Creek acquires Send Technology to strengthen AI-driven underwriting capabilities
item.description: Duck Creek Technologies, a provider of software solutions for property and casualty (P&C) and general insurance, has acquired Send Technology Solutions Ltd, an AI-native underwriting orchestration engine. “By bringing Send into Duck Creek, we are combining core insurance operations and underwriting orchestration in a trusted agentic AI experience that connects systems of record, decision intelligence, workflows, and action. Send will continue to be available as a standalone underwriting orchestration engine that integrates with all core systems, while benefiting from continued investment and deeper integration with Duck Creek’s Intelligent Insurance Cloud and Agentic AI Platform for mutual customers.

item.title: Faster, Fiercer Wildfires Are Testing Evacuation Plans
item.description: Related: Airbnb Must Face LA Lawsuit Alleging Price Gouging During 2025 Wildfires That’s spurred new efforts to map evacuation chokepoints and develop strategies to quickly get people to safety. “Their speed and ferocity can create conditions that overwhelm even the most meticulously crafted plans,” the US government warned in an evacuation guide released in June. and around the world this summer, evacuation is fast becoming an existential challenge.

item.title: Meta Says States Seek $1.4 Trillion in Penalties in August Youth Safety Trial
item.description: Meta Platforms said in a court filing on July 6 that four states were seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties over accusations the company designed its Facebook and Instagram platforms to addict young users and misled the public about their safety. Meta put forward the figure in its response to the attorneys general’s filings on how penalties should be calculated if the states prevailed at trial . The number, which has not previously been disclosed and is close to Meta’s market capitalization of around $1.5 trillion, comes ahead of an August trial in Oakland, California over the claims brought by California, Colorado, Kentucky and New Jersey against the company.

item.title: Organized Jewelry Theft Ringleader in California Handed 16-Year Sentence
item.description: The leader of an organized retail theft ring targeting high-end jewelry stores across Southern California was handed a 16-year prison sentence this week. According to California Attorney General Rob Bonta’s office, which pursued the case, this marks the longest sentence they have secured in an organized retail theft case. The organized retail theft ring targeted more than 14 stores during the day and after hours using smash-and-grab tactics, often in shopping malls throughout the state.

item.title: Ardonagh Launches Axiiem to Digitise Specialty Insurance Trading
item.description: The Ardonagh Group has unveiled Axiiem, a new InsurTech venture designed to connect data, distribution, and capacity across the specialty insurance market through a more data-driven approach to placement. The Ardonagh Group has unveiled Axiiem, a new InsurTech venture designed to connect data, distribution, and capacity across the specialty insurance market through a more data-driven approach to placement. Ardonagh, one of the world’s largest independent insurance distribution platforms and a top 15 global broker, has built Axiiem on proprietary technology developed internally over several years.

item.title: ANV Group Completes Acquisition of Iris Insurance Brokers, Expanding UK Distribution and Lloyd’s Access
item.description: The post ANV Group Completes Acquisition of Iris Insurance Brokers, Expanding UK Distribution and Lloyd’s Access appeared first on Insurtech Insights . ANV Group Holdings has completed its acquisition of London-headquartered Iris Insurance Brokers, broadening its UK retail and wholesale distribution, securing direct access to Lloyd’s specialty capacity, and adding a UK-based MGA incubator. ANV Group Holdings has completed its acquisition of London-headquartered Iris Insurance Brokers, broadening its UK retail and wholesale distribution, securing direct access to Lloyd’s specialty capacity, and adding a UK-based MGA incubator.

item.title: Duck Creek Acquires Send Technology to Combine Intelligent Core with AI-Native Underwriting Orchestration
item.description: The post Duck Creek Acquires Send Technology to Combine Intelligent Core with AI-Native Underwriting Orchestration appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Duck Creek Technologies has acquired Send Technology Solutions, uniting its intelligent core and Agentic AI Platform with Send’s AI-native underwriting orchestration engine to connect core operations and underwriting workflows across the policy lifecycle. Duck Creek Technologies has acquired Send Technology Solutions, uniting its intelligent core and Agentic AI Platform with Send’s AI-native underwriting orchestration engine to connect core operations and underwriting workflows across the policy lifecycle.

item.title: Adaptive Insurance raises $5 million
item.description: Insurance startup Adaptive Insurance announced the close of an additional $5 million in financing from investors IAG Firemark Ventures, Sunna Ventures, Room & Pillar, and Connecticut Innovations, Congruent Ventures, Seraphim Space, and private stakeholders. The round brings Adaptive’s total funding to $10 million. This funding allows us to continue expanding development and distribution of our suite of products and the technology that supports them.” – Mike Gulla, CEO and Co-Founder of Adaptive Insurance.

item.title: WTW enhances UK employee benefits with Everest Funeral Concierge
item.description: WTW has partnered with Everest Funeral Concierge UK to offer funeral planning and bereavement advocacy services through its UK employee benefits brokerage business. The partnership marks the first time Everest’s funeral concierge service has been distributed through a benefits broker in the UK. Employees and their families of WTW’s UK employee benefits clients will gain access to 24/7 telephone support for funeral planning, independent advocacy, cost comparisons, and practical guidance before and after a bereavement.

item.title: Zywave CEO Martin Simoncic: Why Insurance Needs a Different Conversation around AI
item.description: The post Zywave CEO Martin Simoncic: Why Insurance Needs a Different Conversation around AI appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . About Martin Simoncic A software industry veteran, Martin Simoncic joined Zywave as CEO in September 2024 to help lead the company through its next phase of growth. Martin Simoncic sat down with Coverager to talk about why the industry’s AI narrative is broken, and what it actually takes to win.

item.title: Iris Global incorpora a Javier Cano como nuevo director de Operaciones
item.description: Javier Cano Sánchez se une al equipo de Iris Global como nuevo director de Operaciones. Cuenta con una sólida trayectoria de más de dos décadas liderando áreas de operaciones, transformación y experiencia de cliente. A lo largo de su carrera, ha ocupado posiciones de máxima responsabilidad en roles ejecutivos en ámbitos de transformación y operaciones.

item.title: Sabseg refuerza la estructura de su Oficina de Aceleración
item.description: Sabseg refuerza la estructura de su Oficina de Aceleración

item.title: Register: Risky Future AI Tools for Underwriting ‘Demo Day’ on July 8
item.description: In partnership with Insurance Journal, Risky Future’s AI Tools for Underwriting Demo Day will be held on Wednesday, July 8, beginning at 1pm ET. Insurance Journal’s Risky Future series is hosting the “ AI Tools for Underwriting ” Demo Day, a series of free AI tool demonstrations designed exclusively for Carriers, MGAs, Specialty Program Leaders, and of course, Underwriters and Claims professionals. Expect demonstrations around data ingestion + enrichment, risk summaries from messy docs, pricing guidance, and underwriting consistency.

item.title: United Airlines Must Face Lawsuit Over ‘Window Seats’ That Lack Windows
item.description: A federal judge on Monday rejected United Airlines’ bid to dismiss a lawsuit by passengers who complained they paid extra money to sit in window seats, only to discover their seats had no windows. District Judge James Donato in San Francisco rejected United’s defense that “window” referred to the location of a seat relative to the cabin wall and aisle, and that the carrier never contractually promised that seats in the window position would have views outside. (Reporting by Stempel in New York; Editing by Matthew Lewis) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Liberty Names Howell Head of Claims, UK and Mena
item.description: Liberty Specialty Markets named Sarah Howell head of claims for the U.K. Howell will report to Mike Gillett, chief claims officer, Liberty International Insurance, and Luis Prato, president, U.K. Howell will lead the claims function across the U.K.
